revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Gaoshi Shrine]][[fa:معبد گائوشی]][[ja:高士神社]][[vi:Đền Cao Sĩ]][[zh:高士神社]] {{short description|Shinto shrine in Pingtung County, Taiwan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=June 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Gaoshi Shrine | native_name = 高士神社 | native_name_lang = zh | religious_affiliation = Original: [[Shinto]]
Current: None | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:高士神社.jpeg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright = | alt = | caption = | location = Mudan, Pingtung | coordinates = {{Coord|22.1225|N|120.83888888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{plainlist| * Original: [[Amaterasu]]{{cite news |author1=蔡宗憲 |title=高士神社首位台籍神官 黃俊瑜主持例祭 |url=https://news.ltn.com.tw/news/local/paper/1198230 |accessdate=18 May 2020 |work=[[Liberty Times]] |date=6 May 2018 |language=zh-tw}} * Current: None }} | tradition = | sect = | festival = | country = {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|qid=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}} | administration = | consecration_year = | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines#Hokora or Ungraded shrines|Ungraded shrine]] | heritage_designation = | leadership = | ownership = | governing_body = | patron = | website = | architect = | architecture_type = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | creator = | funded_by = | general_contractor = | established = {{plainlist| * First: 1939{{cite news|author1=郭シセン|date=7 May 2018|title=初の台湾人神職が例祭執り行う 「夢への第一歩」に喜び|language=ja|agency={{ill|Central News Agency (Taiwan)|en|Central News Agency (Taiwan)|qid=Q697130|lt=Central News Agency|1=Central News Agency (Taiwan)}}|url=http://japan.cna.com.tw/news/achi/201909150003.aspx|accessdate=18 May 2020}} * Reconstructed: 2015 }} | groundbreaking = | year_completed = | construction_cost = | date_demolished = | facade_direction = | capacity = | length = | width = | width_nave = | interior_area = | height_max = | materials = [[Chamaecyparis obtusa]] wood and concrete | elevation_m = | inscriptions = | elevation_footnotes = | nrhp = | designated = | added = | refnum = | footnotes = | religious_features_label = | religious_features = | location = {{ill|Mudan Township|qid=Q707867|1=Mudan, Pingtung}} }} '''Gaoshi Shrine''' ({{lang-zh|t=高士神社|p=Gāoshì Shénshè}}), formerly known as {{nihongo|'''Kuskus Shrine'''|クスクス祠|Kusukusu Jinjya}}, is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Gaoshi, a {{ill|Paiwan people|en|Paiwan people|qid=Q715238|lt=Paiwan|1=Paiwan people}} village in {{ill|Mudan, Pingtung|en|Mudan, Pingtung|qid=Q707867|lt=Mudan, Pingtung|1=Mudan, Pingtung}}, Taiwan. With the original shrine destroyed by typhoon in 1946, a new shrine was rebuilt in 2015, making it the first Shinto shrine constructed in Taiwan in the post–{{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} era, following the end of {{ill|Taiwan under Japanese rule|en|Taiwan under Japanese rule|qid=Q137816|lt=the island's Japanese rule|1=Taiwan under Japanese rule}}.{{cite news|date=10 August 2015|title=台版「靖國神社」 明牡丹鄉揭幕|language=zh-tw|work=[[Apple Daily]]|url=https://tw.appledaily.com/new/realtime/20150810/667010/|accessdate=18 May 2020}}{{cite news |author1=劉建邦 |author2=程啟峰 |title=陸男網路稱來台毀損高士神社 移民署：未入境 |url=https://www.cna.com.tw/news/asoc/201909140123.aspx |accessdate=18 May 2020 |agency={{ill|Central News Agency (Taiwan)|en|Central News Agency (Taiwan)|qid=Q697130|lt=Central News Agency|1=Central News Agency (Taiwan)}} |date=14 September 2019 |language=zh-tw}} The current shrine is not affiliated with the Shinto religion (or any other deity) but serves as a memorial for the {{ill|Taiwanese people|en|Taiwanese people|qid=Q707133|lt=Taiwanese|1=Taiwanese people}} population lost in wars such as World War II.{{cite news|author1=蔡宗憲|date=11 August 2015|title=祖靈祈福加日本儀式 高士佛神社揭幕|language=zh-tw|work=[[Liberty Times]]|url=https://news.ltn.com.tw/news/life/breakingnews/1409074|accessdate=18 May 2020}} == History == The shrine was originally built in 1939 during the {{ill|Taiwan under Japanese rule|en|Taiwan under Japanese rule|qid=Q137816|lt=Japanese rule of Taiwan|1=Taiwan under Japanese rule}}, but was destroyed in 1946 due to {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|qid=Q140588|lt=Typhoon|1=Typhoon}} damage. The village's residents wished to rebuild the shrine but could not, as reconstruction was prohibited under {{ill|Martial law in Taiwan|en|Martial law in Taiwan|qid=Q4501943|lt=martial law|1=Martial law in Taiwan}} at the time. The only surviving portion of the shrine was its concrete base. In 2015, a ''[[kannushi]]'' named {{nihongo|Ken'ichi Satō|佐藤健一}} learned of the shrine's existence through the Friends of [[:en:Lee Teng-Hui|Lee Teng-Hui]] Association (台灣李登輝之友會總會) in Japan, a cultural exchange organization based in Tokyo.{{cite web |url=http://www.ritouki.jp/ |title=Home |website=ritouki.jp}} Satō decided to rebuild the shrine as a token of appreciation for Taiwan's foreign aid in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake|2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]. The shrine cost {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|qid=Q8146|lt=JP¥|1=Japanese yen}}10,000,000 to build. An opening ceremony was held on 11–12 August 2015 to celebrate the shrine's completion. It involved a Shinto ceremony conducted by Satō, traditional Paiwan dances, and a prayer led by the local pastor, since the majority of Gaoshi's population is {{ill|Christianity in Taiwan|en|Christianity in Taiwan|qid=Q712877|lt=Christian|1=Christianity in Taiwan}}. == Structure == No records of the original structure exist. The rebuilt shrine is made of [[hinoki]] wood, while the roof is covered in copper. In 2016, a white ''[[torii]]'', also made of hinoki, was added in front of the shrine.{{cite news|author1=張茂森|date=12 April 2016|title=日人捐贈屏東高士神社大鳥居 5/7豎立|language=zh-tw|work=[[Liberty Times]]|url=https://news.ltn.com.tw/news/world/breakingnews/1661947|accessdate=18 May 2020}}{{cite web|title=想看絕美鳥居，不必大老遠飛日本！全台7處仙境大公開，靠海純白鳥居實在太夢幻啦|url=https://www.storm.mg/lifestyle/345507|author1=葉亭儀 |date=19 October 2017|website=The Storm Media|language=zh-tw|accessdate=18 May 2020}} == See also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Taiwan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Taiwan|qid=Q6596888|lt=List of Shinto shrines in Taiwan|1=List of Shinto shrines in Taiwan}} * {{ill|Japanese invasion of Taiwan (1874)|en|Japanese invasion of Taiwan (1874)|qid=Q703942|lt=Japanese invasion of Taiwan (1874)|1=Japanese invasion of Taiwan (1874)}} * [[:en:Luye Shrine|Luye Shrine]] == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinmei shrines}} [[File:Mudan-CHO_YEN_CHIA-IMG_1330.jpg|thumb|The shrine's white ''torii'']] [[en:Gaoshi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kuskus Shrine}} [[fa:معبد گائوشی]] [[ja:高士神社]] [[vi:Đền Cao Sĩ]] [[zh:高士神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21449537}} [[Category:1939 establishments in Taiwan]] [[Category:1946 disestablishments in Taiwan]] [[Category:2015 establishments in Taiwan]] [[Category:20th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:21st-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Monuments and memorials in Taiwan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Pingtung County]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taiwan]] [[Category:Taiwan under Japanese rule]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
